name: Dutrac Community
description_short: DuTrac Community Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution founded in 1946, operating 14 branches across Iowa and Illinois with full banking services.
description_long:
DuTrac Community Credit Union was established in 1946 and has grown to serve the Dubuque, Iowa area and surrounding regions with a network of 14 physical branch locations. As a credit union, the organization operates on a cooperative, member-owned model rather than as a profit-driven bank, with a stated mission to make quality financial services accessible to everyone in its communities.

The institution offers core banking services including checking and savings accounts, credit and lending products, and ATM access across its branch network. Their physical presence spans Iowa cities (Dubuque, Bettendorf, Davenport, Clinton, Dyersville, Eldridge, Maquoketa, and Monticello) and extends into Illinois with a location in Moline. Most branches maintain dual lobby and drive-up service windows during business hours.

DuTrac distinguishes itself through its community-focused branch network and long operational history. The credit union emphasizes accessibility with extended hours at multiple locations—some branches open as early as 7:30 AM and stay open until 5:30 PM on weekdays, with a Walmart branch offering evening hours until 6:00 PM. The organization provides interactive location tools and branch locators to help members find convenient access points.

As a traditional credit union with geographically limited operations, DuTrac is best suited for individuals living or working in its service areas who value in-person banking and community-oriented financial institutions. The website provided focuses on branch locations and basic service categories rather than detailed product information, rates, or comparative advantages. Consumers should contact DuTrac directly to evaluate specific account terms, lending rates, and membership eligibility requirements. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: ATM access and cash withdrawal services
Drive-up window banking at most locations
pros: 14 branch locations providing extensive geographic coverage across Iowa and Illinois
Extended drive-up hours at main offices (7:30 AM opening at Asbury Road main office)
Walmart branch location in Dubuque offering evening hours until 6:00 PM
Founded in 1946 with 75+ years of operational history and community presence
Interactive branch locator tools and ATM finder for member convenience
Multiple service windows (lobby and drive-up) at most locations
Credit union structure emphasizing member ownership and community accessibility
cons: Service area limited to Iowa and Illinois—not available for customers in other states
Website lacks detailed information about account types, rates, fees, or loan products
No online account opening or comprehensive digital banking information visible
Limited competitive rate and product information available for comparison
Smaller institution with fewer resources and services compared to national banks
best_for: Residents and employees in the Dubuque, Iowa metropolitan area seeking community banking
Individuals in Iowa and Illinois who prefer credit union membership and in-person banking services
Members prioritizing extended branch hours and convenient physical access to banking services
diagnosis: DuTrac Community Credit Union is best for individuals living or working in its service territories across Iowa and Illinois who value community-based banking with physical branch access and a cooperative credit union structure. The primary caveat is that this is a geographically limited institution not available outside its service areas, and detailed product information, rates, and competitive offerings are not accessible from the provided website—direct contact with DuTrac is necessary for account evaluation.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
